title: Delicious Coca Cola
wikitext:
[]: Delicious Coca-Cola             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Aug 08, 2014 12:11 am They actually included a name brand soda in one scene. Most references to real world brands in cartoons are either paid promotions ( Peanuts ) or jokes ( Family Guy ). I don't think this qualifies as either, so it seems a bit odd to me. I mean, I know it's not a joke, so could it be product placement? Or did someone just feel like drawing Coke?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Aug 08, 2014 12:22 am Asashi is also a brand name.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Aug 08, 2014 1:29 am Ah. The only beer brand I can name is Budweiser.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Aug 08, 2014 6:51 am Similar things happen in American cinema. Mission Impossible 4 featured a lot of Apple products, and yet never took in a single cent for the placement of those products. (Confirmed on the director's twitter.) I've worked on many a film where we had to call a company to get permission to feature a product in our movie, but never went so far as to get compensation for it. The more money they throw at you for advertising their products, the more control the get over how they're featured in your movie, and we didn't want that. Meanwhile Man of Steel tries to off set spending (on top of the over $200 million production budget) an extra $150 million in advertising by placing the products from over 5 different companies in the movie. So yeah, not every time we see a product in a movie does it mean that it's paid product placement. It just means that someone put a product there for some reason. I don't think there was any paid placement in NGE outside of Sega, though the NTE series seems to have had some with at least Doritos and Pizza Hut. Everything else in those series appears to be simply details of the world the characters live in.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Aug 08, 2014 11:49 am There are lots of real world brands in the show. Some of them were removed for the on-air broadcast (most notably, the "Yebisu" beer cans from Misato's appartment says "Yebichu" after the Ebichu manga, but was changed back to "Yebisu" for the home video release). DHL boxes when Asuka arrives, not to mention all of the brand names of booze: Cointreau, Campari, Pernod, Dekuyper White Curacao, etc that wasn't removed for the TV broadcast.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Aug 08, 2014 11:56 am I thought part of the name change from Yebisu to Yebichu was because the network was annoyed at having unpaid product placement in the show, and complained?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Aug 08, 2014 12:42 pm JAE FAQ wrote: The name of Misato's beer was changed to Yebichu when it was aired on TV and then to Yebisu for the Video release.* … Yebisu … is definitely a real beer. However, after complaints by the brewers, in episode 2 the name was changed to "Yebichu", which may also be a homage to a manga series, "Oruchuban [Ebichu]". According to Carl Horn the brand name was retracted because the TV network which broadcast the series objected because the brewers of "Yebisu" did not advertise with them, and didn't want to give the brewers free publicity!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Aug 08, 2014 12:58 pm Thanks for the full story, Ornette!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Aug 12, 2014 4:38 pm Dunno. Am I the only one who finds it more realistic than bland grey cans?

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Tue Aug 12, 2014 5:13 pm Don't forget! Next year the Sega Saturn is going to make a big comeback and dominate the console market!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 12, 2014 9:29 pm Was Boa a real brand of bear? I remember that name in there as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 12, 2014 9:54 pm I thought that had to do with "Flying Phantom Ship": Don't think there's a brand of beer called "Boa", closest thing the internet has is a dive bar in Rio called "Bar da Boa" where they have their logo on beer glasses and haven't been around for all that long.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Aug 13, 2014 10:43 am Well at least now we know, that Coca Cola survived Second Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Aug 13, 2014 11:40 am Thank God.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Aug 13, 2014 11:44 am You can (not) kill it. McDonald's is still around as well, probably. Keepin' them people fat and tasty.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Aug 13, 2014 1:42 pm Well here's the thing, the animators at Gainax wanted to give the world of Eva a psuedo realistic flair. But their sponsors were fickle. Hence why they had to change the name of the Yebisu to Yebichu halfway through, and BOA when they couldn't even swing that. BOA is a now defunct brand of Soda that existed back in the 60's-70's. The animators being the nerds they are decide to throw the defunct brands of their childhood in the background for both visual appeal and to give the world a sense of realism without having to actually have that company sponsoring them.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Aug 13, 2014 5:12 pm You'd think that a movie from the 1969 would never get away with calling their main plot device (which kills people) "BOA Juice".

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Aug 13, 2014 8:38 pm Just remember, it could be worse. Evangelion: IGN Edition wrote: "Erm, Ritsuko..." Shinji said, nervously, as a sickly yellow fluid began to flood the entry plug. "What's happened to the LCL? It...it looks like piss." "Ah, about that..." the scientist sighed. "Our budget's been running kinda low, so we had to resort to selling out to popular brands- and one of the contracts stipulated that we had to replace the LCL with Mountain Dew..." The pilot groaned, wincing as he was submurged in the additive-riddled soft drink. "Yeurch, it tastes like calpol- oh god, what is this hideous orange paste that's flooding in?" "...That'll be the other contract;" replied Ritsuko. "Pump the entry plugs with liquidized Doritos at regular intervals...sorry, should have mentioned that..." She turned to face her assistant. "Maya, could you start the launch sequence?" she said, tiredly. "I need to get a McDonald's McFlurry tm ...God I hate this job..." "Sure!" replied Maya, struggling to maintain her cheery facade as she faced a newly installed rectangular camera. "Xbox, launch Evas."

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Aug 14, 2014 3:38 am ^ Well, if that means we get NGE with the animation quality of Q... Not sure if gusta.

I mean, I know it's not a joke, so could it be product placement? Or did someone just feel
